treatment_information.dosage_or_details: 20 mg IV, followed by another 10 mg
treatment_information.treatment_response: Blood pressure improved from 167/110 to 146/88 after first dose.

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: Paralytics: 20 mg Etomidate IV, 60 mg Rocuronium IV, followed by 40 mg Rocuronium IV
treatment_information.treatment_response: Patient became less responsive and required intubation.
Patient remained paralyzed for an additional period.
Provided nutrition due to swallowing difficulties.

treatment_timeline.event: Patient presented with severe headache and left-sided weakness.
Patient treated with IV Mannitol and blood pressure medications.
Intubation performed due to decline in mental status.
Patient placed on IV Nicardipine drip and re-imaging conducted.
PEG tube placed for nutrition management.
Antibiotics administered for urinary tract infection.

patient_outcome.follow_up_details: Follow up with Neurology and primary care physician.
patient_outcome.long_term_prognosis: Not specified, but ongoing monitoring required.
patient_outcome.quality_of_life: Patient requires assistance and is bedbound.
patient_outcome.patient_perspective: Patient experienced the worst headache of life with subsequent stroke symptoms; not explicitly recorded beyond treatment course.
